overrides.override_summary: Termination prior to expiration only for good cause. Franchisee must be given written notice and 30 days to cure.
overrides.why_it_matters: Michigan franchisees have stronger protection against arbitrary termination than the standard FA provides.

overrides.override_summary: Franchisor must fairly compensate franchisee on non-renewal by repurchase or fair market value of inventory/supplies/equipment/fixtures (if term <5 years and franchisee prohibited from continuing under another brand or less than 6 months notice).
overrides.why_it_matters: Provides economic protection on non-renewal, though 20-year term may limit applicability of the <5 year trigger.

overrides.override_summary: Provision requiring arbitration/litigation outside Michigan is void. However, franchisor states it intends to enforce the arbitration clause regardless and believes Michigan law is unconstitutional on this point.
overrides.why_it_matters: Creates legal uncertainty for Michigan franchisees. Franchisor's stated position directly contradicts Michigan statute.

overrides.override_summary: Franchisor may not refuse to permit transfer except for good cause. Right of first refusal permitted.
overrides.why_it_matters: Limits franchisor's discretion to block transfers.

overrides.override_summary: Cannot require franchisee to assent to release/waiver depriving rights under the Act, but post-entry settlement permitted.
overrides.why_it_matters: Renewal/transfer release requirements may be unenforceable in Michigan.

overrides.override_summary: Franchisee may bring action in Maryland for claims under Maryland Franchise Registration and Disclosure Law, unless preempted by FAA.
overrides.why_it_matters: Maryland franchisees may avoid Chicago forum for state-law claims.

overrides.override_summary: No waiver/disclaimer of reliance provision supersedes other terms.
overrides.why_it_matters: Protects against fraudulent inducement claims being waived.

overrides.override_summary: 90 days notice of termination (with 60 days to cure) and 180 days notice for non-renewal required per Minn. Stat. §80C.14.
overrides.why_it_matters: Longer notice and cure periods than standard FA provides.

overrides.override_summary: Release on renewal/transfer will not apply to extent prohibited by Minn. Rule 2860.4400D.
Cannot require litigation outside Minnesota. Cannot abrogate franchisee's rights under Minnesota Franchises Law including right to Minnesota courts.
overrides.why_it_matters: Strong protection of Minnesota franchisee's right to home-state forum.

overrides.override_summary: Minn. Rule 2860.4400J prohibits waiving jury trial rights, consenting to liquidated damages, termination penalties, or judgment notes. Franchisor states it will enforce to extent law allows.
overrides.why_it_matters: Liquidated damages clause and jury waiver may be unenforceable in Minnesota.

overrides.override_summary: ND Commissioner determined termination/liquidated damages to be unfair, unjust, and inequitable under §51-19-09. Franchisor will enforce to extent law allows.
overrides.why_it_matters: Liquidated damages provisions may be unenforceable in North Dakota.

overrides.override_summary: Release will not apply to extent prohibited by ND Franchise Investment Law.
overrides.why_it_matters: Release requirements on renewal/transfer may be limited.

overrides.override_summary: Arbitration at mutually agreed site (not necessarily Chicago) to extent required by ND law unless preempted by FAA. Franchisee may bring action in North Dakota.
overrides.why_it_matters: Potential home-state arbitration for ND franchisees.

overrides.override_summary: North Dakota law applies to extent required by law.
overrides.why_it_matters: Illinois choice of law may not apply in North Dakota.

overrides.override_summary: Forum selection provision restricting venue outside Rhode Island is void for claims under RI Franchise Investment Act §19-28.1-14.
overrides.why_it_matters: RI franchisees protected from out-of-state litigation for state-law claims.

overrides.override_summary: Illinois law generally applies except as required by law for claims under RI Franchise Investment Act.
overrides.why_it_matters: RI franchise law claims governed by RI law.

overrides.override_summary: Virginia Retail Franchising Act §13.1-564: Unlawful to cancel franchise without reasonable cause. Grounds not constituting reasonable cause may not be enforceable.
overrides.why_it_matters: Virginia franchisees have statutory protection against unreasonable termination.
